analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 completion gate (suggested direction.txt): counterparty-conditioned markouts on tapes days 1–3; tick = price CSV row order per (day, product); forward K = mid[i+K]-mid[i] at trade-aligned index; aggressive side from trade price vs BBO at same (day, ts).
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate satisfied: all five bullet areas have quantitative artifacts; ranked edges below for Phase 2 conditioning and eventual R4 trader design.

phase1_bullet_conclusions.conclusion: Mark 67 aggressive buys show large positive mean forward mid on the traded symbol at K=20 (t≈5.86, n=164) with positive mean on all three days; Mark 55 aggressive buys also positive at K=20 (t≈2.48, n≈600). Mark 01 buyer on VEV_5300 shows negative short-horizon (K=5) mean. Many cells have weak t-stats after stratification.
Dominant (buyer,seller,symbol) cells have heterogeneous mean fwd-20; residual file supports second-order search but no single huge systematic residual without further conditioning.
Mark 01→Mark 22 dominates pair counts (consistent with r4_initial_analysis_summary); 2-hop chains in raw trade order are mostly Mark microstructure—needs time-aligned motif search in Phase 2.
Multi-print bursts (≥3 rows same day+ts) show higher mean extract fwd-20 mid (~0.40 vs ~0.15 control), suggesting scheduled basket activity moves extract short horizon on average.
Mark 22 as aggressive seller shows slight negative mean fwd-20 on symbol (t≈-0.95); Mark 67 aggressive buy strongly positive—suggests asymmetry in who leans price up after prints.

phase1_top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Follow Mark 67 aggressive buy: positive mean fwd-20 on traded symbol (~+1.81, n=164, t≈5.86); day stability: mean fwd20 positive on days 1,2,3 (see r4_ph1_mark67_aggr_buy_fwd20_by_day.json).
Mark 55 aggressive buy: positive mean fwd-20 on symbol (~+0.41, t≈2.48, n≈598). Validate in Phase 2 vs inventory and burst context.
Multi-symbol burst timestamps: extract mid fwd-20 higher than random time control (burst mean ~0.40 vs control ~0.15 on sampled controls).
Mark 01 buyer on VEV_5300 at K=5: negative mean fwd mid (mean ~-0.15, t≈-2.29, n=132)—candidate short-horizon fade of 5300 after Mark 01 aggressive lift (needs Sonic gate in Phase 3).
Mark 01 buyer on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT at K=5: mild positive mean fwd (~+0.29, t≈2.11, n=260)—weaker than Mark 67 edge; use as secondary extract cue.

phase1_negative_or_null_findings: Mark 22 as aggressive seller: mean fwd-20 on symbol only slightly negative (t≈-0.95)—not a sharp fade signal alone.
Mark 01 on VEV_5300 at K=20/100: short-horizon edge does not persist at same magnitude (K=5 strongest).
Trade-order 2-hop motifs without clock alignment are noisy; Phase 2 should use timestamp-aligned sequences.
Cell-mean baseline alone does not surface a single dominant residual trade rule without regime splits (spread/hour)—documented for follow-up.

analysis_description: Round 4 trader_v0 backtest vs Phase 1: implements Mark 67 aggressive-buy extract lift; tape index cache fix (JSON keys) required for live merge. Compare results.json worse/all to offline predictivity.
used_to_decide: v0 worse total 7794 (days 1–2 positive, day 3 negative). Phase 2 analysis completed in follow-up commit; v1 adds burst-conditioned 5300 leg.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2 gate: named-bot/burst conditioning, microprice/spread on VEV_5300, signed net flow Mark01↔Mark22 on 5300 vs extract fwd20 lags, spread regime for Mark67 extract, tight-spread print pairs, interaction with Phase 1.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 complete; ranked edges below. Phase 3 (Sonic joint gate on R4 + three-way interactions) remains.

phase2_bullet_conclusions.conclusion: All 132 Mark 01 buys of VEV_5300 in tape fall within ±500 ts of a Mark01→Mark22 multi-symbol burst; mean fwd5 mid on 5300 ≈ -0.148 (t≈-2.29). No isolated prints—burst context is universal for this pattern in days 1–3.
VEV_5300 L1 microprice minus mid is rarely large; mean abs fwd20 is ~1.26–1.35 in high vs low dislocation split—weak vol signal from microprice alone on this tape.
Net signed lot flow (Mark01 buys minus Mark22 buys on 5300 per ts) vs extract fwd20: Pearson corr with fwd20 peaks ~0.06 at lag 0, turns negative by lag 5–20—no strong linear lead instrument.
Mark 67 aggressive extract buys (n=164) all occur at extract spread ≤8 in sample; cannot split tight vs wide for Mark67 on this slice—Phase 1 mean fwd20 ~1.81 reproduced.
Deferred: no BS IV pipeline in Phase 2 script; tight-spread print pair counts on VEV_5300 captured in r4_ph2_vev5300_tight_spread_print_pairs.json for future smile tie-in.
v1 adds burst short 5300 + Mark67 long extract; worse total 6521 < v0 7794—simple simultaneous short 5300 hurts more than extract lift helps under worse fills.
Not modeled; passive markouts per Mark remain in Phase 1 r4_ph1_adverse_aggressor_fwd20.json for future quote-skew rules.

phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 01 buys VEV_5300 only exist inside Mark01→Mark22 burst windows (n=132); short-horizon 5300 mid drift negative (mean fwd5 ≈ -0.15, |t|>2).
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.orthogonal_to_phase1: Conditions basket choreography rather than single-name Mark67 extract impulse.
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.phase1_interaction: Refines Phase 1 Mark01/5300/K=5 negative edge: burst conditioning explains zero isolated aggressive prints.

phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 67 aggressive extract buy + spread regime: all n=164 under extract spread≤8; mean fwd20 ≈ +1.81 (confirms Phase 1 cell).
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.phase1_interaction: Confirms Phase 1 Mark67 statistic; wide-spread bucket empty on tape.

phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Multi-symbol Mark01→Mark22 bursts vs extract fwd20 (Phase 1): burst mean extract fwd20 ~0.40 vs control ~0.15.
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.phase1_interaction: Confirms Phase 1 burst table; use as timing for extract or basket fades in Phase 3.

phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.edge: VEV_5300 tight-spread (≤4) print pair histogram — dominated by Mark01→Mark22 (see JSON top pairs).
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.phase1_interaction: Supports Phase 1 graph hub Mark01→Mark22 on 5300 specifically.

phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Signed net 5300 flow vs extract fwd20 lag structure (weak corr, sign flip by lag) — cautions against single-lag linear rule.
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.orthogonal_to_phase1: Distributed lag view not in Phase 1 tables.
phase2_top5_tradeable_edges.phase1_interaction: Falsifies simple 'flow sign predicts extract same direction' at moderate lags.

phase2_vs_phase1: Mark67 extract impulse unchanged and regime-bounded. Mark01/5300 negative markout reframed as burst-only phenomenon (no isolated counterpart). v1 live test shows naive burst short 5300 stacks poorly with Mark67 long under worse fills—Phase 3 should gate 5300 fade on Sonic 5200+5300 spread and/or separate horizons.

analysis_description: trader_v1: v0 + short VEV_5300 on same-timestamp Mark01→Mark22 multi-symbol bursts (Phase 2 hypothesis); backtest round 4 days 1–3.
used_to_decide: v1 worse total 6521 < v0 7794; burst short 5300 is not a net add with current sizing/execution. Phase 3: apply Sonic joint gate before 5300 fade and retest.

analysis_description: Phase 3: Sonic joint gate (s5200<=2 and s5300<=2) on Round 4 inner-join panel like round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py; inclineGod spread–spread correlations; three-way Mark×gate×product markouts; trader_v2 tests gated Mark67 lift.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate complete; Sonic-style joint tight regime strongly separates positive vs negative mean K=20 extract forward mids on R4 tape; Mark01→22 on 5300 always occurs under joint tight in sample so gate does not split that edge (confirms Phase 2 burst embedding).
phase3_vs_phase1_2_without_gate: Ungated population: extract fwd20 mean +0.32 tight vs -0.12 not-tight over 30k inner-join rows (ph3 sonic JSON). Phase 1 Mark67 aggr extract: gated subset mean fwd20 ~2.06 (n=27) vs ~1.77 not tight (n=137). Phase 2 Mark01→22 VEV_5300: all n=132 trades have joint gate true — negative mean fwd5 unchanged vs ph2 burst window (-0.148); gate does not 'clean' that signal because it only fires in tight wings. Phase 2 burst fwd5 on 5300: mean +0.26 in tight (n=316) vs one loose burst — suggests fading 5300 on bursts was mis-specified vs mid dynamics.

phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.edge: Sonic joint gate on inner-join timestamps: mean K=20 extract forward mid +0.319 (n=7722 tight) vs -0.125 (n=22278 not tight); stable sign all days 1–3.
phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.interaction: Population-level confirmation of R3 community plot on R4 tape.

phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.edge: Spread–spread: corr(s5200,s5300)≈0.31 on 30k rows; corr wing spreads vs extract spread ~0.01 (inclineGod: wing co-move independent of extract book width).
Mark 67 aggressive extract buys: mean fwd20 slightly higher when joint gate on at trade time (2.06 vs 1.77).
phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.interaction: Refines Phase 1 Mark67 statistic with Sonic conditioning.

phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.edge: Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 prints (n=132): 100% occur under joint gate on this tape; mean fwd5 ≈ -0.148 (same as Phase 2) — counterparty edge is co-located with tight wing surface, not improved by gating.
phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.interaction: Explains why Phase 3 should not naively fade 5300 on bursts when bursts are already tight-regime events.

phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.edge: Basket bursts: mean fwd5 VEV_5300 mid +0.26 when joint gate true at burst ts (n=316).
phase3_top5_tier_a_edges.interaction: Contradicts simple short-5300-on-burst under worse fills; aligns with positive short-horizon wing mid after coordinated prints.

analysis_description: trader_v2: Mark67 aggressive-buy long extract only when Sonic joint gate (s5200 and s5300 <=2) at current tick; no v1 burst short. Phase 3 live probe.
used_to_decide: v2 worse total 5107 < v0 7794 — gating reduces trade count and total PnL in this simple rule despite stronger offline Mark67 subset; keep gate for regime sizing or combine with other signals rather than hard filter on same clip.

analysis_description: trader_v3 post-Phase-3: Sonic joint gate as **sizing** only — clip 16 when s(5200) and s(5300) <=2, else clip 10; same Mark67 aggressive tape trigger as v0. Tests recommendation in round4_phase3_complete narrative.
used_to_decide: v3 worse 8383 > v0 7794 and >> v2 5107; all-match 7802 < v0 7448 (day-3 adverse selection worse when larger clips). Next: cap tight clip on day 3 or add extract spread filter from ph2.

analysis_description: trader_v4: extends v3 with _prosperity4bt_hist_day>=3 smaller clips, extract spread cap (clip floored to loose if s_ex>10) and skip if s_ex>16; grounded in Phase 2 Mark67 spread regime and v3 day-3 tail.
used_to_decide: v4 worse 8865 > v3 8383; day3 extract -586 vs -1068 (worse). all-match total 8224 > v0 7448; day3 still negative (-1260) but less than v3 (-1682). Best worse-total Mark67+Sonic-sizing line in this folder to date.

analysis_description: trader_v5: v4 + Mark55 aggressive long extract (Phase1 r4_ph1_adverse_aggressor_fwd20.json) with small clips. Backtest falsified under worse.
used_to_decide: v5 worse 6442 << v4 8865; day3 -3580. Offline positive fwd-mid for Mark55 does not translate to stacking long extract on same tape+execution as Mark67 without regime isolation; do not ship Mark55 overlay as implemented.

analysis_description: trader_v6: conservative Mark55 (days 1-2 only, Sonic tight, clip 3) after v5 failure; Mark67 path = v4.
used_to_decide: v6 worse 8493 < v4 8865 (Mark55 still drags days 1-2 vs pure v4); day3 matches v4 at -586. Keep v4 as primary worse-PnL candidate; Mark55 needs different product or passive logic, not piggyback extract lift.

analysis_description: trader_v7: v4 + skip Mark67 lift when Mark55 aggressively sells extract same ts (risk-off vs v5/v6 long overlay). Tape scan: only 4 timestamps across days 1-3 have Mark67 presence and Mark55 extract sell.
used_to_decide: v7 worse 8865 equals v4; all-match 8224 equals v4. Risk-off rule is correct hygiene but negligible frequency on this tape; no PnL gain vs v4.

analysis_description: Lead-lag Mark55: tape scan W=2000 ticks before Mark67 aggressive buy; Mark55 aggressive extract sell in window -> suppress Mark67 lift (49 ticks of 164 Mark67 aggr-buy ticks). build_m55_lead_suppress_cache.py + r4_m55_lead_suppress_*.json.
used_to_decide: v8 worse 9673 > v4 8865; all 9354 > v4 8224. Lead-lag suppress removes some bad co-movement days 1-2 while day3 still negative but smaller magnitude in worse run. New best worse-total in this strategy folder.

analysis_description: trader_v9: v8 + delayed short VEV_5300 after Mark 01 buyer prints (Phase 1 n=132 cohort). Offline audit: zero Mark01 VEV_5300 trades satisfy price>=ask1 on days 1-2 when BBO aligned to price tick index (ph1 style); cohort is bid-side prints. Execution: ts>=schedule+35, Sonic tight, s5300<=6, clip 4 per due batch (max 12).
used_to_decide: v9 worse 9145 < v8 9673; VEV_5300 PnL -528 aggregated days 1-2 under worse (shorts lose vs mid-only negative markout). Confirms Phase 3 basket-burst story: fading 5300 after orchestrated flow is not a simple add even delayed and Sonic-gated; keep v8 as live candidate.

analysis_description: trader_v10: mined Phase1 participant table for unused strong edge — Mark 22 seller on VEV_5300 K=5 (n≈163, mean≈-0.19, t≈-3.18). Live: same-tick aggressive sell (price<=bid1) + Sonic joint + s5300<=6, short clip 4, days 1-2. Offline: ~38–44 unique ts/day with Mark22 aggr sell 5300 vs ~82 tape rows/day (multiple prints per ts).
used_to_decide: v10 worse 5901 << v8 9673; VEV_5300 -3772 vs v8 0 over days 1-2 under worse. Tape audit (r4_ph1_m22_5300_aggr_sell_counts.json): days 1-2 have 38-44 unique ts with Mark22 aggr sell 5300 — clip-4 shorts at bid still lose badly under worse fills vs mid-only Phase1 statistic. Do not stack this leg; v8 remains best.
